nodes.context: Chinese secret society and triad prominent in Shanghai in early-mid 20th century, involved in opium, extortion, gambling, prostitution.
Buddhist sect founded by Luo Qing in mid-Ming dynasty, precursor to the Green Gang.

aliases: Friends of the Way of Tranquility and Purity
nodes.context: Direct precursor to the Green Gang in the early 20th century, organized by boatmen and salt smugglers in northern Jiangsu.
Nationalist political party led by Chiang Kai-shek, allied with the Green Gang.
Ethnic Chaozhou syndicates that took over the heroin market in Hong Kong after the Green Gang's decline.

edges.context: The Green Gang has its roots in the Luojiao Buddhist sect.
The Anqing Daoyou was the direct precursor to the Green Gang.
The Green Gang was hired by Chiang Kai-shek's Kuomintang to break up strikes and shared drug profits.
The Green Gang suffered in struggles against local Chaozhou syndicates over control of the drug market in Hong Kong.
